facilities.name: Village of Wilmette Storm Water Pumping Station (SWPS)
facilities.note: 4000 Lake Avenue, on Wilmette's western border. Village-owned (not MWRD) -- lifts storm/street runoff from the fully separated storm sewer system serving west Wilmette (~2.8 sq mi west of Ridge Road, built out 1930-1950) and discharges by gravity to the North Branch Chicago River near the Lake Avenue overpass. Five pumps, run automatically on intake-chamber sump-level signals; combined capacity 585 cfs per a 2020 Village hydrologic/hydraulic study. Monitored remotely from the Village's C.P. Dubbs Water Treatment Plant; staff dispatched during significant rainfall. Switchgear (last replaced 1991) and one pump's VFD (installed 2001) replaced under a $1.93M contract approved Oct. 2022, substantially complete late 2023. Not to be confused with MWRD's own Wilmette Pumping Station (id wilmette-ps in facilities.json), the lakefront North Shore Channel controlling works ~0.7 mi east.
facilities.source: Village of Wilmette, 'Storm Water Pumping Station' <https://www.wilmette.gov/326/Storm-Water-Pumping-Station> (address, service area, gravity discharge point, operations/monitoring); 'Sewer System Management' <https://www.wilmette.gov/369/Sewer-System-Management> (confirms single Lake Ave. pumping station serves all of west-of-Ridge-Road drainage); 'Trustees OK $1.9M upgrade to stormwater pumping station...' The Record North Shore, Oct 12 2022 <https://www.therecordnorthshore.org/2022/10/12/trustees-ok-1-9m-upgrade-to-stormwater-pumping-station-and-get-first-look-at-2023-budget-with-property-tax-reduction/> (1936 build date, 5 pumps, switchgear/VFD history, $1.93M contract); Village of Wilmette 'Neighborhood Storage Project' presentation to the Stormwater Committee, Nov 6 2020 <https://archive.metroplanning.org/uploads/cms/documents/csc_presentation_wilmette_stormwater.pdf> (585 cfs capacity, 2.8 sq mi service area, built-out dates), local copy sources/cso-outfalls-discharges/2020-village-of-wilmette-neighborhood-storage-project-presentation.pdf. Coordinate cross-checked against OpenStreetMap building tagged 'Stormwater Pumping Station,' 4000 Lake Avenue, Wilmette (way 888722844, nominatim.openstreetmap.org). See docs/14-cso-outfalls-discharges.html, 'Non-MWRD stormwater pumping stations' subsection. Accessed July 2026.

connections.label: Lake Michigan water admitted through MWRD's Wilmette Pumping Station into the North Shore Channel
connections.source: Schematic route, not a surveyed centerline. Endpoints/waypoints: an approximate Lake Michigan point east of Wilmette Harbor; the North Shore Channel's own head-of-channel vertex and a downstream vertex, both taken directly from this archive's existing map-data/waterways-modern.json 'north-shore-channel' geometry; and the Wilmette Pumping Station coordinate (42.0755,-87.685), which matches both the existing map-data/facilities.json 'wilmette-ps' marker and an independent OpenStreetMap building geocode ('1912 Wilmette Pumping Station,' Sheridan Road, way 1373401864) to 4 decimal places. Represents the lake-diversion function documented in docs/02-river-reversal-canals.md and docs/07-mwrd-interceptors-pumping-stations.md (Wilmette PS built 1908-1910 as the North Shore Channel's controlling structure). Accessed July 2026.

connections.label: Village of Wilmette SWPS -- gravity discharge to the North Branch Chicago River near the Lake Avenue overpass
connections.source: Schematic; Village of Wilmette <https://www.wilmette.gov/326/Storm-Water-Pumping-Station> states discharge flows 'by gravity to the river, near the Lake Avenue overpass' but publishes no surveyed outfall coordinate. End point is interpolated on this archive's existing map-data/waterways-modern.json 'north-branch-chicago-river' geometry at the latitude nearest the station's OSM-confirmed coordinate (way 888722844, 4000 Lake Avenue). Accessed July 2026.
